concerns.location: spec.md:User Story 3 - Predictive Modeling and Robustness Validation
concerns.text: The spec contains a critical text corruption in User Story 3. The acceptance scenario 3 is interrupted mid-sentence ('Given the rarefaction depth threshold...') and immediately followed by a completely unrelated research question: 'The research question is: How does gut microbiome composition differ between individuals with and without diagnosed autoimmune disease?' This redefines the project's core scientific question from 'cognitive decline' to 'autoimmune disease', constituting a fatal drift from the source idea.

concerns.location: spec.md:User Story 3 - Predictive Modeling and Robustness Validation
concerns.text: The spec includes a citation '(Segata et al., 2012) https://doi.org/10.1038/nature11230' in the middle of the corrupted text block. This citation refers to 'Metagenomic biomarker discovery and explanation' (often associated with gut microbiome and disease, but not specifically the autoimmune question inserted). The insertion of this unrelated research question and citation breaks the logical flow and scope fidelity to the 'Cognitive Decline' idea.

concerns.location: spec.md:User Story 3 - Predictive Modeling and Robustness Validation
concerns.text: User Story 3 Acceptance Scenario 3 is incomplete and syntactically broken. It starts with 'Given the rarefaction depth threshold...' but fails to complete the 'When... Then...' structure due to the insertion of the unrelated research question text. This makes the requirement untestable as written.
